description_long: This is a large, amphora-style terracotta vase with a wide, rounded body that tapers toward a narrow base. The neck is short and cylindrical, flaring outward at the rim, and is flanked by two robust, curved handles that connect the rim to the shoulder of the vase. The surface is matte and unglazed, with a natural, earthy brown color and subtle textural variations typical of hand-shaped clay. There are no visible decorative motifs or embellishments, giving it a utilitarian yet classic appearance. The vase appears hollow and capable of holding water or other contents. Its substantial size and sturdy construction suggest it could serve as a decorative or functional object in an outdoor or public urban setting.
description: A large, amphora-shaped terracotta vase with two curved handles and a matte, earthy brown surface. The form is simple and unadorned, emphasizing its classic, utilitarian style.
description_view_0: Front view showing both curved handles symmetrically attached to the neck and shoulder, with the wide body and flared rim clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view revealing only one handle and the smooth, uninterrupted curve of the vase's body, highlighting the vase's depth and the thickness of the neck.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying both handles and the broad, rounded body, with the rim and neck in full view.
description_view_3: Opposite side view to view 1, again showing a single handle and the continuous, smooth surface of the vase's body.
